methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: Tech Briefs: New RAMP cohort members introduce themselves
signals.signal_reading: RAMP cohort expansion in historically underserved regions combined with VT library AI research grants signals emerging distributed capability-building infrastructure, though depth remains to be proven through implementation outcomes.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's formalized partnership with AI Ready RVA concentrates educational resources and innovation capacity in Richmond's urban core, creating a metro hub model that may deepen the capability gap with rural Virginia unless explicitly designed for regional diffusion.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads businesses adopting AI tools represents platform-enabled shallow adoption—broad implementation without necessarily building deep technical capability or local AI development expertise.

signals.title: Report says 35% of the jobs in Virginia may be at risk to artificial intelligence. Southside the least impacted, Northern Virginia the most. - Cardinal News
signals.signal_reading: The 35% job displacement risk with Northern Virginia most exposed and Southside least impacted reveals a paradox: regions with deepest AI capability face greatest disruption while underserved areas remain insulated by lack of AI penetration—neither distribution nor meaningful depth materializing equitably.

signals.title: Fairfax ramps up efforts to address AI’s impacts on economy, future workforce - FFXnow
signals.signal_reading: Fairfax County's systematic workforce planning for AI impacts demonstrates sophisticated institutional response capacity that only well-resourced metro jurisdictions can deploy, further concentrating adaptive capability in NoVA while rural regions lack equivalent planning infrastructure.
